was not a choice. It was the only way because there is no way you could send doctors to these remote medically underserved locations. So we had to think very differently. So to make it easier as an anesthesia you eat more bitlen nuts. So you're accelerating the process. Now we created a very interesting product. And when we say blue collar obviously the definition is very fluid but you can think of people as lowkilled right. So people who let's say work in a factory or your security guard or the pe the the person who delivers your food these days right these are all blue collar let's say what we found was that and as you mentioned the hiring ecosystem for blueco collar workers in India is very very fragmented in fact it turns out that there are hundreds of thousands of local recruitment agencies that do a bulk of the hiring in this space and that's also because trust is a very very key element for people in general and especially people from Bhat when they're searching for a job. So you will usually find that you know people will look for jobs through their mama cha network or through a local recruitment agency. So the question is how do you use technology to maybe replicate that trust or or leverage that trust? it turns out that it's not really possible and so what we've done instead is we've built what was termed as an augmentation sort of approach earlier where we've aggregated over 3,000 human recruiters who work at local agencies on our platform across over 900 cities in India and we are providing our AI recruiter as a tool to these human recruiters. So the the human recruiter will typically source somebody who's looking for a job on the ground perhaps locally and then they will enter their information into our platform which is when our AI recruiter takes over. It's it gives them a call. It's able to talk to them in multiple Indian languages. it's able to ask them basic questions what is their name, where are they from, what are they looking for, any educational background. it matches them with a job. It's able to

takes over. It's it gives them a call. It's able to talk to them in multiple Indian languages. it's able to ask them basic questions what is their name, where are they from, what are they looking for, any educational background. it matches them with a job. It's able to always salary. So, obviously, you know, those types of questions and then it proceeds from there to the onboarding process, interviewing process, etc. And anywhere where we see a candidate dropping off or showing signs of of slowing down, that's when we reintroduce the human recruiter. Right? This is called a human in the loop system where you bring the human back into the the automation or the the workflow. and we're finding that that is absolutely critical again in a place like India where trust is incredibly important to deliver services especially services that are high stakes or that are complex. a human plus AI approach works much much better than an AI approach or a human only approach. In fact, we find that if a human recruiter was able to get, let's say, one person hired a day, when they use the AI, they can get five hired a day. And this is still very very early days. The technology is absolutely in its nency. In 2 to 5 years, that five could become 50. That's our goal, right? So, we're able to help the human recruiter obviously earn more money because they are much more productive. more people are able to get jobs in a shorter amount of time and of course employers are able to hire more in a shorter amount of time as well.
